entry_before_open_analysis: Entry-before-open is structurally impossible: ball_entry_evaluation_into_destination_region scans ball frames from open_frame_id forward, so the first in-region frame satisfies frame_id >= open_frame_id. time_to_entry_seconds is therefore always >= 0.0, and entry_mode is PRESENT_AT_OPEN exactly when the entry frame equals the open frame (time_to_entry_seconds == 0.0).

entry_mode_audit.entry_before_open_analysis: Entry-before-open is structurally impossible: ball_entry_evaluation_into_destination_region scans ball frames from open_frame_id forward, so the first in-region frame satisfies frame_id >= open_frame_id. time_to_entry_seconds is therefore always >= 0.0, and entry_mode is PRESENT_AT_OPEN exactly when the entry frame equals the open frame (time_to_entry_seconds == 0.0).

capability_gaps: No APPROVED recipe exactly matches possession-anchored progressive corridor opening plus destination-region ball entry. The closest support appears to require an EXPERIMENTAL typed plan.
clarification_questions: Which match_ids should the default invocation bind to?
first_turn_decision.notes: I searched Priori tactical recipes and found experimental corridor support, but submit_query_plan requires a concrete default invocation scope: non-empty match_ids, non-empty periods, and perspective_team_role as home or away. I stopped before execution.

arguments.query: progressive corridor opens possession starts remains available ball enters destination region
progressive corridor opens possession starts remains available ball enters destination region

classification_rules.description: A progressive corridor opened within four seconds of possession start, persisted for at least 0.8 seconds, and the ball entered the selected corridor destination region within five seconds of corridor opening.
classification_rules.label: DESTINATION_ENTERED

allowed_claims: The team had an active-ball possession anchor.
A geometric progressive corridor appeared under configured relation thresholds within the configured post-possession-start window.
The corridor remained available for at least the configured duration.
The ball entered the selected corridor destination region within the configured post-open horizon.
recipe.description: Experimental composition from ordinary possession-start anchors: detect geometric progressive corridors opening within the configured post-possession-start window, require configured open duration, and classify whether the ball enters the selected corridor destination region within the configured post-open horizon.
disallowed_claims: The attack intentionally created the corridor.
A pass should have been played.
The corridor is optimal.
The result is a pass probability, decision-quality, causation, player-intent, or missed-opportunity claim.
The result is backed by licensed match video.
limitations: Experimental plan; not an approved recipe.
Destination entry is geometric ball-region entry only.
No offside, receiver body orientation, pressure, pass probability, or optimality model is evaluated.
No match video is used.

parameters.description: Maximum time after possession start for the progressive corridor to open.
parameters.name: corridor_max_window_seconds

parameters.description: Minimum forward progression for a corridor segment.
parameters.name: corridor_minimum_progression_m

parameters.description: Minimum source-target segment length.
parameters.name: corridor_minimum_segment_length_m

parameters.description: Maximum source-target segment length.
parameters.name: corridor_maximum_segment_length_m

parameters.description: Minimum defender clearance from the source-target segment.
parameters.name: corridor_minimum_clearance_m

parameters.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered open.
parameters.name: corridor_open_after_frames

parameters.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered closed.
parameters.name: corridor_close_after_frames

parameters.description: Minimum time the corridor remains available.
parameters.name: corridor_minimum_duration_seconds

parameters.description: Maximum time after corridor opening for ball entry into the corridor destination region.
parameters.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

hero_question.text: Show possessions where a progressive corridor opens within four seconds of possession starting, remains available for at least 0.8 seconds, and the ball enters that corridor's destination region within five seconds of the corridor opening.

classification_rules.description: A progressive corridor opened within four seconds of possession start, persisted for at least 0.8 seconds, and the ball entered the selected corridor destination region within five seconds of corridor opening.
classification_rules.label: DESTINATION_ENTERED

allowed_claims: The team had an active-ball possession anchor.
A geometric progressive corridor appeared under configured relation thresholds within the configured post-possession-start window.
The corridor remained available for at least the configured duration.
The ball entered the selected corridor destination region within the configured post-open horizon.
recipe.description: Experimental composition from ordinary possession-start anchors: detect geometric progressive corridors opening within the configured post-possession-start window, require configured open duration, and classify whether the ball enters the selected corridor destination region within the configured post-open horizon.
disallowed_claims: The attack intentionally created the corridor.
A pass should have been played.
The corridor is optimal.
The result is a pass probability, decision-quality, causation, player-intent, or missed-opportunity claim.
The result is backed by licensed match video.
limitations: Experimental plan; not an approved recipe.
Destination entry is geometric ball-region entry only.
No offside, receiver body orientation, pressure, pass probability, or optimality model is evaluated.
No match video is used.
